questions.q: Singer-actor K. L. Saigal is most famous for his work in which area of the arts?
questions.explanation: K. L. Saigal was a singer-actor who became the defining singing star of 1930s Indian cinema.

questions.q: In which year was K. L. Saigal born?
questions.explanation: Kundan Lal Saigal was born on 11 April 1904.

questions.q: In which year did K. L. Saigal die?
questions.explanation: K. L. Saigal died on 18 January 1947 at the age of 42.

questions.q: With which famous Kolkata-based film studio was K. L. Saigal primarily associated during his rise to fame?
questions.explanation: Saigal rose to fame at New Theatres in Calcutta (Kolkata).

questions.q: K. L. Saigal's celebrated portrayal of the title character in which 1935 film made him the era's most influential vocalist?
questions.explanation: His performance as the title character in Devdas (1935) cemented his stardom.

questions.q: In the era before the playback system took hold, how did K. L. Saigal's songs reach the screen?
options: They were mimed to another singer's voice
They were dubbed after his death
He sang them himself on screen
questions.explanation: Saigal sang his own songs on screen, before pre-recorded playback singing became standard.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian union territory was K. L. Saigal born?
questions.explanation: Saigal was born in Jammu, in the present-day union territory of Jammu and Kashmir.

questions.q: K. L. Saigal acted and sang in films made primarily in which two languages?
questions.explanation: He worked extensively in both Hindi and Bengali cinema of the 1930s and 1940s.

questions.q: K. L. Saigal is widely remembered with which title reflecting his place in film history?
questions.explanation: He is celebrated as the first singing star of Indian cinema.

questions.q: Roughly how old was K. L. Saigal when he died?
questions.explanation: Born in 1904 and died in 1947, Saigal was 42 years old at his death.

questions.q: Which New Theatres music director, a pioneer of Indian film music, composed many of K. L. Saigal's early hit songs?
questions.explanation: Rai Chand (R. C.) Boral, a pioneer of film music at New Theatres, composed many of Saigal's early hits such as those in Street Singer and President.

questions.q: In the 1935 film Devdas, K. L. Saigal played the title role of Devdas; which actress played the heroine Paro?
questions.explanation: Jamuna played Parvati (Paro) opposite Saigal's Devdas in the 1935 New Theatres production.

questions.q: Which composer worked with K. L. Saigal on his final film Shahjehan (1946), producing hits like 'Jab Dil Hi Toot Gaya'?
questions.explanation: Naushad composed the music for Shahjehan (1946), including 'Jab Dil Hi Toot Gaya', the song Saigal cherished most.

questions.q: The famous song 'Babul Mora Naihar Chhooto Hi Jaye', sung by K. L. Saigal, featured in which 1938 New Theatres film?
questions.explanation: Saigal sang 'Babul Mora' in Street Singer (1938), a thumri attributed to Wajid Ali Shah.

questions.q: Which blind saint-poet did K. L. Saigal portray in the 1942 New Theatres devotional film Bhakta Surdas?
questions.explanation: Among his devotional roles, Saigal portrayed the blind saint-poet Surdas in Bhakta Surdas (1942).

questions.q: In which Indian city did K. L. Saigal die in 1947?
questions.explanation: Saigal died in Jullundur (Jalandhar), Punjab, in January 1947.

questions.q: Before becoming a film star, K. L. Saigal famously worked for a time as a travelling salesman for which kind of product?
questions.explanation: Saigal worked as a salesman for the Remington typewriter company before entering films.

questions.q: In the 1943 film Tansen, K. L. Saigal played the legendary court musician Tansen; which actress played his co-star Tani?
questions.explanation: Khursheed Bano starred opposite Saigal in Tansen (1943), one of the biggest musical hits of its time.

questions.q: Which later legendary playback singer openly idolised K. L. Saigal and sang a small part alongside him in Shahjehan (1946)?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Rafi, a devoted admirer of Saigal, sang in the chorus of Shahjehan (1946) early in his career.

questions.q: K. L. Saigal's hit song 'Ek Bangla Bane Nyara' was featured in which 1937 New Theatres film?
questions.explanation: 'Ek Bangla Bane Nyara' featured in President (1937), one of Saigal's popular films of the period.

questions.q: In the later years of his career, K. L. Saigal shifted his base from Calcutta to which city to work in the Hindi film industry?
questions.explanation: Saigal moved to Bombay in the early 1940s, where he made films such as Tansen, Bhakta Surdas and Shahjehan.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor did K. L. Saigal portray in his final film, the 1946 musical Shahjehan?
questions.explanation: In Shahjehan (1946), Saigal played the Mughal emperor Shah Jahan, builder of the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: What was K. L. Saigal's full given name?
questions.explanation: His full name was Kundan Lal Saigal, often abbreviated as K. L. Saigal.

questions.q: Which 1932 film is generally regarded as K. L. Saigal's screen debut as a leading actor?
questions.explanation: Saigal's debut films in 1932 included Mohabbat Ke Aansu, made under New Theatres.

questions.q: Which New Theatres composer, also a noted singer, mentored K. L. Saigal and gave him songs in films like Dushman and Doctor?
questions.explanation: Pankaj Mullick, a key New Theatres music director and singer, mentored Saigal and composed his songs in Dushman and Doctor.

questions.q: K. L. Saigal recorded several non-film songs, including ghazals of which Urdu poet that became closely linked with his voice?
questions.explanation: Saigal's renditions of Mirza Ghalib's ghazals are among his most cherished non-film recordings.

questions.q: In which 1934 New Theatres film did K. L. Saigal sing songs composed by R. C. Boral, scoring an early success that established his fame?
questions.explanation: Chandidas (1934) was an early New Theatres success that boosted Saigal's standing as a singing star.

questions.q: The song 'Diya Jalao Jagmag Jagmag', associated with K. L. Saigal, comes from which of his films?
questions.explanation: 'Diya Jalao' was a celebrated Saigal number from Tansen (1943), set to music by Khemchand Prakash.

questions.q: Which song is widely said to have been played at K. L. Saigal's own funeral, fulfilling a wish of his?
questions.explanation: 'Jab Dil Hi Toot Gaya' from Shahjehan is famously associated with Saigal's funeral in 1947.

questions.q: The lullaby 'So Ja Rajkumari', sung by K. L. Saigal, featured in which 1938 New Theatres film?
questions.explanation: Saigal's tender lullaby 'So Ja Rajkumari' featured in Street Singer (1938).
